title: psidium variabile
parts: Psidium variabile has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, roots, and fruits, which are used in traditional medicine. The leaves of Psidium variabile are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of this plant are known to contain sesquiterpenes and triterpenes, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The fruits of Psidium variabile are a good source of ascorbic acid, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
side_effects: Psidium variabile can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, including skin irritation, itching, and redness, particularly when the leaves or sap come into contact with the skin. Ingesting large amounts of the plant's leaves or fruits can lead to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged use of Psidium variabile can also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and decrease their effectiveness. Additionally, improper use of the plant may cause an imbalance in electrolytes, leading to symptoms like muscle weakness, fatigue, and dizziness.
